title: Coordinator: Facilities
description:
About the Firm

Mayer Brown is an international law firm positioned to represent the world’s major corporations, funds, and financial institutions in their most important and complex transactions and disputes. We are recognized by our clients as strategic partners with deep commercial instincts and a commitment to creatively anticipating their needs and delivering excellence in everything we do.

We are a collegial and collaborative firm where highly motivated individuals with an unwavering commitment to excellence receive the opportunity, support, and development they need to grow, thrive, and realize their greatest potential all while supporting the Firm’s client service principles of excellence, strategic partnership, commercial instinct, integrated strengths, innovation, and collaboration across our international firm.

If you enjoy working with team members whose defining characteristics are exceptional client service, initiative, professionalism, responsiveness, and adaptability, you may be the person we are seeking to join our Administration and Facilities department in our Singapore office, as a Coordinator: Facilities.

The Role

Responsible in the areas of office services, mailing services, document destruction, company event support and any supervisor assigned tasks to ensure the operation services are maintained at the high standard.

  • Handle mails and courier services (internal and external documents / parcels) when required.
  • Assist in managing document distribution to ensure quality of services being delivered to the stakeholders and accurate record.
  • Assist in setting up and resetting the conference rooms.
  • Repair and maintenance of office equipment.
  • Responsible for internal staff relocation/moving.
  • Assist in furniture fixing/maintenance.
  • Assist in procurement process and outdoor searching if required.
  • Assist on daily operation including security system, maintenance, cleaning services and staff enquiries, requests and complaints.
  • Participate in ad hoc duties as assigned by supervisor/manager.

Qualification and Experience

  • Minimum secondary school education.
  • Customer-oriented and able to work under pressure with multi-tasking ability.
  • Eager to learn, willing to handle outdoor work and work under pressure
  • Work independently, efficiently and flexible with strong sense of responsibility.
  • Highly flexible to work beyond office hours.
  • Strong communication skills.
  • Good command of written and spoken English.
  • Proficiency in PC operation in MS Word, MS Excel and PowerPoint is preferred.

Monday to Friday, 9.00 a.m. to 6.00 p.m. with flexibility to work additional hours in accordance with the needs of the business.

At Mayer Brown, we are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive work environment that offers our people the opportunity and support they need to succeed. We are therefore happy to discuss any reasonable adjustments that individuals may require throughout the recruitment process and once they have joined the Firm.

In addition, one of our core values at Mayer Brown is to promote diversity and inclusion at all levels within the business which is actively supported by our diversity networks - LGBT+, Fusion (BAME), Women, and Work and Me (Family).

#LI-DNI
